list_searches.summary: Get a list of all your searches

list_companies.summary: Get a list of all your companies

list_workspaces.summary: Get a list of all your workspaces

list_tokens.summary: Get a list of all your API tokens

search_count.summary: Get an approximate count of results for the search over a particular period

list_tags.summary: Get a list of all your tags

post_/v3/tags.description: Create a new tag for tagging documents. Tag names must be unique and a maximum of 128 characters.

delete_/v3/tags/{tag_id}.description: Deletes a given tag. Any documents that possess this tag will have it removed following tag deletion.

add_tags.description: Triggers a request to add a list of specified tags to a given set of documents. This is an asynchronous operation.

remove_tags.description: Triggers a request to remove a list of specified tags from a given set of documents. This is an asynchronous operation.

list_filter_sets.summary: Get a list of all search filter sets

list_custom_categories.summary: Get a list of all search custom categories

getV3Analytics-searchId-start-end-tz-sou.summary: A summary of analytics for a Saved Search.
getV3Analytics-searchId-start-end-tz-sou.description:
The summary is produced only for documents matching the Saved Search,
from the specified document sources, and within the specified time-range.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents that are included in the analysis.

The `unique_authors` value can only be calculated for sources other than `news`.
When run against `news` it will return 0.

getV3AnalyticsTop_tags-searchId-start-en.summary: The top tags that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_tags-searchId-start-en.description:
Produces a ranked list of tags that occur in documents matching the
Saved Search, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top tags produced,
defaulting to the top `10` tags.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents that have their tags included.

The type of tags included, such as Twitter "hashtags", will depend on the
selected sources.

getV3AnalyticsTop_locations-searchId-sta.summary: The top locations that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_locations-searchId-sta.description:
Produces a ranked list of locations that occur in documents matching the
Saved Search, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top locations produced,
defaulting to the top `100` locations.

The level parameter allows you to select the level of location you want from country, state or city.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents that have their keyphrases included.

getV3AnalyticsTop_shared-searchId-start-.summary: The top shared documents that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_shared-searchId-start-.description:
Produces a ranked list of documents matching the Saved Search that were
the most shared, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top shared documents
produced, defaulting to the top `10` top shared documents.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents that are included.

The `sort_by` parameter controls the ranking of the results. For example,
if `sort_by=retweets`, the results will be the top N documents by retweet
count. Retweet results are based on when the retweet occured, not the
creation date of the original tweet

getV3AnalyticsTop_entities-searchId-star.summary: The top named entities that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_entities-searchId-star.description:
Produces a ranked list of entities that occur in documents matching the
Saved Search, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top keyphrases produced,
defaulting to the top `100` keyphrases.

The sentiment parameter allows you to select the sentiment of the documents to analyze.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents that have their keyphrases included.

getV3AnalyticsTop_sources-searchId-start.summary: The top sources that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_sources-searchId-start.description:
Produces a ranked list of "sources" that provided the documents matching the Saved Search.

For social sources, a "source" will be the author of that document.

The `size` parameter controls the maximum number of sources to produce, defaulting
to the top `10` sources.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents whose sources will be produced.

getV3AnalyticsTop_keyphrases-searchId-st.summary: The top keyphrases that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_keyphrases-searchId-st.description:
Produces a ranked list of keyphrases that occur in documents matching the
Saved Search, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top keyphrases produced,
defaulting to the top `100` keyphrases.

The sentiment parameter allows you to select the sentiment of the documents to analyze.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents that have their keyphrases included.

getV3AnalyticsTop_mentions-searchId-star.summary: The top mentions that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_mentions-searchId-star.description:
Produces a ranked list of mentions that occur in documents matching the
Saved Search, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top tags produced,
defaulting to the top `10` mentions.

getV3AnalyticsTop_topics-searchId-start-.summary: The top topics that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_topics-searchId-start-.description:
Produces a ranked list of topics and their sub topics that occur in documents matching the
Saved Search, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top topics produced,
defaulting to the top `10` mentions.

getV3AnalyticsTop_sharedLinks-searchId-s.summary: The top shared links that occur in a Saved Search.
getV3AnalyticsTop_sharedLinks-searchId-s.description:
Produces a ranked list of links that occur in documents matching the
Saved Search, from the specified document sources, and within the
specified time-range.

The `size` parameter controls the number of top shared links  produced,
defaulting to the top `100` links.

Any other parameters provided, such as language or country, will further
restrict the documents that have their links included.

post_/v3/analytics/{searchId}/custom.description:
Create a custom analytics request with your choice of filters and analytic types and configuration.

Please see example requests/responses below or our guide on Custom Analytics [here](/guides/listening/analyzing-mentions) for more information.

get_/v3/analytics/custom/catalog.summary: Fetch the latest custom analytics options
get_/v3/analytics/custom/catalog.description: Fetch the latest dimensions, measures, and analytic types you can use to create your custom analytic requests.

list_onetime_exports.summary: Get a list of all your one-time exports

show_onetime_export.summary: Get details of a one-time export

list_recurring_exports.summary: Get a list of all your recurring exports

show_recurring_export.summary: Get details of a recurring export

handleByocDocuments.description: Handles the import of documents into the Meltwater Platform.

get_/v3/imports/batches.description: Retrieves a list of import batches. Supports filtering by date range (start/end) and batch status. Dates are interpreted in the specified timezone (defaults to UTC).

get_/v3/imports/batches/{batch_id}.description: Retrieves status and statistics about an import batch.

get_/v3/imports/import_tags/{import_tag}.description: Retrieves status and statistics about an import tag.

create-earned-search.summary: Search for Meltwater earned data using a saved search and optional filters.
create-earned-search.description: Search for Meltwater earned data using a saved search and optional filters.

get_/v3/usage/me/metrics/{metric}.summary: Get usage information on specific metric
get_/v3/usage/me/metrics/{metric}.description: Get usage information on specific metric

get_/v3/usage/me/requests.summary: Get usage statistics for your API calls
get_/v3/usage/me/requests.description: Get usage statistics for your API calls

get_/v3/owned/accounts.summary: Returns a list of social account connections for a given company/user account
get_/v3/owned/accounts.description: Returns a list of social account connections for a given company/user account

get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/breakdown.summary: Returns a single-level breakdown by a category or concept
get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/breakdown.description: A 'breakdown' metric is an analysis that provides a single-level breakdown by a category or concept. For example, the `demographics_audience_country` metric breaks down the audience for a Facebook page by country. All timestamps in this endpoint work on PST timezone (Meta's preferred timezone).

get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/heatmap.summary: Returns a break-down over date and time
get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/heatmap.description: A 'heatmap' metric is an analysis that provides a break-down over date and time. For example, the `page_fans_heatmap` metric breaks down the audience for a Facebook page by how active they are each day and hour. All timestamps in this endpoint work on PST timezone (Meta's preferred timezone).

get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/nested_br.summary: Returns a double-level breakdown by two categories or concepts
get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/nested_br.description: A 'nested-breakdown' metric is an analysis that provides a double-level breakdown by two categories or concepts. For example, the `demographics_gender_age` metric breaks down the audience for a Facebook page by firstly gender, then age group. All timestamps in this endpoint work on PST timezone (Meta's preferred timezone).

get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/numeric.summary: Returns simple metrics for a social account
get_/v3/owned/accounts/metrics/numeric.description: A numeric metric is the most basic type of metrics you can get for your social media account, like the number of page fans. All timestamps in this endpoint work on PST timezone (Meta's preferred timezone).

get_/v3/owned/accounts/posts/top_posts.summary: Allows to fetch the top posts for owned social accounts
get_/v3/owned/accounts/posts/top_posts.description: Allows to fetch the top posts for owned social accounts. You might do this to report the most engaged with posts in a period of time for example. Timestamps in this endpoint for Facebook and Instagram sources work on PST timezone (Meta's preferred timezone).

get_/v3/owned/supported_metrics.summary: Returns a list of supported metrics

getAllHooks.description:
List all hooks.

Delivers all previously generated hooks.

createHook.description:
Creates a hook for one of your predefined searches.
 Delivers search results for the query referenced by the `search_id` to the `target_url` via HTTP POST. Note that a `hook_id` will be returned on successful creation, this id is needed to delete the hook.

 We are also returning the header: `X-Hook-Secret`, a shared secret used to sign the document body pushed to your `target_url`.


 Specify `company_id` if your `search_id` is not in your default company.

getHook.description:
Get an individual hook.

Retrieves an existing hook.

deleteHook.description:
Delete an existing hook.
 Removes the hook and stops sending any search results to the target_url.

post_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_fiel.summary: Create a new custom field value.
post_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_fiel.description: Create a new custom field value.

get_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_field.summary: Fetch a given custom field value.
get_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_field.description: Fetch a given custom field value.

put_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_field.summary: Update a given custom field value.
put_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_field.description: Update a given custom field value.

delete_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_fi.summary: Delete a given custom field value.
delete_/v3/explore_plus/assets/custom_fi.description: Delete a given custom field value.

post_/v3/explore_plus/analytics/custom.summary: Analyse earned data in your private index.
post_/v3/explore_plus/analytics/custom.description: Analyse earned data in your private index.

post_/v3/explore_plus/search.summary: Search for data in your private index.
post_/v3/explore_plus/search.description: Search for data in your private index.

post_/v3/mira/chat.description:
Processes chat completion requests and returns responses in streaming or non-streaming mode.
If thread_id is provided, it will use that thread. If not, it will create a new thread or use the latest one.
If X-Project-ID header is provided, the thread will be associated with that project for enhanced context.
The thread ID is returned in the Thread-ID response header.

post_/v3/mira/responses.description:
Processes chat responses requests with multiple input messages and returns responses in streaming or non-streaming mode.
If Thread-ID header is provided, uses the existing thread; otherwise creates a new thread with all messages and passes only the last user message to Mira.
The thread ID is returned in the Thread-ID response header.

get_/v3/mira/projects.description: Retrieve all projects available to the authenticated user with pagination support
